name: Holo Jupyter
summary: JupyterLite with a bundled Python (Pyodide) kernel — scientific notebooks, fully in your browser, verified.

description.p: Computational notebooks shouldn't need a server, an install, or an account — just a link.

The full JupyterLite distribution runs entirely on your hardware: CPython compiled to WebAssembly (Pyodide) with the scientific stack — NumPy, SciPy, SymPy, pandas, Matplotlib, scikit-learn — bundled locally and content-addressed. Ternary, quantum, and geometric experiments are all expressible here.

ul: Full JupyterLab, Notebook 7, and REPL interfaces
Python kernel in the browser via Pyodide (WASM)
NumPy / SciPy / SymPy / pandas / Matplotlib bundled offline
No server, no install — 100% your hardware
Notebooks persist in your own browser storage

releases.description: JupyterLite 0.7.6 with the Pyodide 0.29.3 kernel and the scientific stack bundled locally — Lab, Notebook 7, and REPL, all content-addressed and offline.

tools.name: holo_jupyter_run
tools.description: Execute Python in the sealed Holo Jupyter environment (Pyodide/WASM: numpy, scipy, pandas, sympy, statsmodels, cvxpy+clarabel, scikit-learn, cirq, backtrader, mplfinance, kingdon, ...). Returns stdout, result, and a kappa-addressed PROV-O receipt (code kappa -> output kappa); deterministic & reproducible (Law L5). For agent copilot and autonomous research.

installs.description: Optional bundled packages to make importable (e.g. cirq-core, backtrader, kingdon)

tools.name: holo_research_run
tools.description: Run an AUTONOMOUS research study in the warm Holo Jupyter kernel: a Python `step` is executed repeatedly (state persists across steps) and the loop adaptively continues until a chosen metric crosses a threshold, then SEALS a citable kappa-addressed report linking the whole ordered step chain + conclusion (a reproducible kappa-DAG). The `step` code must set result = json.dumps({...metrics...}); accumulate across steps using persistent variables (set up in `init`). For agent-driven autonomous research.

goal.description: Plain-language research goal (recorded in the sealed report)

init.description: Optional Python run once to set up persistent state (imports, accumulators)

step.description: Python for one experiment iteration; must set result = json.dumps({...metrics...}); accumulate via persistent variables

metric.description: Key in the step's metrics JSON to watch for the stopping criterion (e.g. stderr)

threshold.description: Stop when the metric crosses this value

comparator.description: How to compare metric to threshold (default <)

installs.description: Optional bundled packages to make importable (e.g. cirq-core, kingdon)
